Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary, Issue, Evidence, And Or Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff Norma Contreras For Disobedience Of Discovery Order Compelling Response To Form Interrogatories, Set 1; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Gary A. Berticevich
Off Calendar Jan-25-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT CEMEX CONSTRUCTION MATERIALS PACIFIC LLC's Motion For Monetary, Issue, Evidence, And Or Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff Norma Contreras For Disobedience Of Discovery Order Compelling Response To Form Interrogatories, Set 1.

Appearances (via Zoom):
Gregory C. Cattermole, Esq., Law Office of, (650) 345-6811, for Plaintiffs.
Gary A. Berticevich, Esq., of De Poel, Levy, Thomas, Arneal, LLP, (925) 934-6102, for Defendant.

Plaintiff and Defendant stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem Judge. The pro tem Judge, Plaintiff, and Defendant (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The matter is argued. At conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. As the moving party has withdrawn the motions insofar as they seek terminating or evidentiary/issue sanctions, the motions for monetary sanctions remain. City of Los Angeles v. PricewaterhouseCoopers, LLC, 84 Cal. App. 5th 466 (2022) holds that C.C.P. 2023.030 does not authorize, in and of itself, monetary sanctions. While the California Supreme Court has granted review of the PricewaterhouseCoopers case, it is nevertheless citable, and trial courts are free to decide whether to follow the holding of this case per the Supreme Court. See: https://appellatecases.courtinfo.ca.gov/search/case/dockets.cfm?dist=0&doc_id=2417020&doc_no=S277211&request_token=NiIwLSEmLkw3W1BBSCM9TEpIIFw0UDxTJCMuTztTICAgCg%3D%3D Therefore, the motions for monetary sanctions are denied WITHOUT prejudice.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported (302/JPT)
